habitat: Open habitats: grasslands; deserts; marshes; agricultural fields; and farmlands
story: In the diverse world of birds, the Barn Owl (Tyto alba) stands out. This species, part of the Tytonidae family, is a resident of Open habitats: grasslands; deserts; marshes; agricultural fields; and farmlands. Its IUCN Red List status is Least Concern (LC), which serves as a call to action for habitat preservation. The Barn Owl is more than just a bird; it is a symbol of the rich biodiversity that we must protect. Through this poster, we hope to foster a greater understanding and appreciation for the Barn Owl and its role in the ecosystem.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
